Document:
# New California Republic (NCR)
Founded in the ruins of Shady Sands in California, the New California Republic is the largest democratic state in the post-war world. Built on the ideals of pre-war America — representative government, rule of law, free trade — it has grown into a sprawling bureaucratic power now stretched thin across the Mojave and beyond.
The NCR arrived in this region seeking influence, trade agreements, and another flag planted for the Republic. Whether their presence is welcome depends entirely on who holds power locally.
## Philosophy
The NCR believes in democracy, organized law enforcement, and expansion by diplomacy first and force second. Critics point to the Republic's worst traits: corruption, political short-sightedness, and a habit of promising protection it cannot reliably deliver at the frontier. Supporters say it remains the best foundation for lasting civilization the post-war world has managed to build.
## Chain of Command
### Officers
[bold]Captain[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Command the NCR's local detachment. All military and diplomatic decisions run through you. Balance force, political dealings, and the Republic's interests — your choices determine whether the NCR gains a foothold in Wendover or retreats in failure.
[bold]Lieutenant[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Second-in-command. Support the Captain, manage day-to-day operations, and step into command if the Captain is unavailable. You are the bridge between officer intent and the NCOs who execute it.
[bold]Medical Doctor[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Senior medical officer attached to the NCR detachment. Keep soldiers combat-capable, treat civilian allies to build goodwill, and advise command on medical threats. Your skills are a diplomatic asset as much as a survival one.
### Non-Commissioned Officers
[bold]Sergeant First Class[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Senior NCO of the detachment. You translate officer intent into enlisted action — enforcing discipline, managing formation readiness, and carrying the load between command and the soldiers who carry out its orders.
[bold]Staff Sergeant[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Senior squad leader. Lead your troops in the field, coordinate with the Sergeant First Class, and handle the mid-level decisions that keep small-unit operations running. You have enough rank to make things happen without waiting for permission.
[bold]Sergeant[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Squad leader. Know your soldiers, execute the Staff Sergeant's direction, and make the right call when there is no time to ask. NCR troopers live or die by Sergeant-level judgment.
### Enlisted
[bold]Specialist[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Trained specialist serving in combat, engineering, or medical roles as needed. Pick your loadout to match the mission — whether that means holding the line with heavy weapons, building fortifications, or keeping soldiers alive in the field. Your versatility is what makes the NCR work.
[bold]Private First Class[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Experienced private. You have proven you can be relied on — better gear, slightly more latitude, a little more trust from the NCOs above you. Hold the line and keep moving up.
[bold]Private[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Standard soldier. Enforce NCR authority, protect personnel and assets, and carry out orders. The politics are above your grade — your job is to be where you are told and do it well.
[bold]Recruit[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Newest enlistee. You are at the bottom of a very long ladder. Follow orders, learn the ropes, and avoid getting yourself or your squadmates killed. Everything else comes later.
### Special
[bold]Prisoner[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Held in NCR custody pending processing or release. What happens to you next depends on what you did, who is in command, and how cooperative you decide to be.
## Relations
The NCR's primary objective is to bring this region under Republic jurisdiction — through diplomacy first, economic leverage second, and military pressure last. They will seek allies wherever they can find them, but the Republic's interests come first.
